rag_rules: Place implementation-level AGREE annex blocks only in implementation scopes, not type scopes.
Use 'assign' in implementation annexes only for existing outputs or declared auxiliary variables.
generic_rules: Preserve AADL structure by restricting AGREE annex content to visible features and declared variables.
Maintain scope integrity: implementation contracts must not reference subcomponents outside their owning scope.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annexes use syntax: annex agree {** ... **};
Implementation-level bindings require 'assign' for outputs; avoid 'guarantee' in implementation scope.
anti_patterns: Do not reference AADL properties (e.g., Period, Deadline) as AGREE variables unless explicitly exposed as ports or constants.
Do not invent declarations for unresolved identifiers; use only visible scope elements.
